text: The surgeon observes adhesions between the cecum and appendix within the inguinal hernia sac.
The surgeon grasps the mesoappendix and pulls it out, dividing the visible attachments to the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon has mostly extracted the appendix from the indirect space and is carefully dissecting additional adhesions to clearly identify the appendiceal mesentery.
The surgeon attempts to create a window in the tissue to facilitate the placement of a ligature around the appendiceal mesentery, which is positioned away from the surgeon's view.
The surgeon creates a window in the tissue and slides the ligature through it to resect the remaining meso-appendix.
The surgeon addresses residual adhesions around the appendix, clearing the area to prepare a clean base for ligation or amputation.
The surgeon places an endo loop at the base of the appendix, opting to avoid using staplers for this appendectomy.
The surgeon divides the appendix at its base, completing the separation from the meso-appendix.
The surgeon cauterizes the exposed mucosa of the appendix stump. The stump is left as it is without imbrication.
